name: Chicken Fried Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare and cut all ingredients as needed.
2. Heat a wok with 1 tablespoon of oil and fry an egg until it is cooked into a thin sheet. Remove the egg to a plate and set aside.
3. Add another 2 tablespoons of oil to the hot wok. Allow it to smoke, then stir-fry chicken and onion together until fully cooked. If using leftover chicken, simply heat through. Add bean sprouts, peas, and celery, stirring for 2 minutes with the wok covered. Uncover the wok and add the remaining vegetables. Stir-fry for an additional 2 minutes while covered.
4. Add the cooked rice to the wok. Season with salt, pepper, and soy sauce. Reduce heat to medium. Use a turner to break up clumps of rice as finely as possible, ensuring that it is well coated with soy sauce and does not remain white. Continue stir-frying until all rice is thoroughly broken down and heated through.
5. Turn off the heat. Chop the egg into small pieces and gently mix into the rice, or transfer the rice to a serving platter and garnish with egg slices.
NOTE: Using leftover rice results in better fried rice. If using leftover rice, rinse it under hot water from the tap before cooking. Chicken, pork, beef, or shrimp (cooked or uncooked) can be substituted for chicken.
